event: Sign and Light — Abstract Cross Composition
symbols: red cross at center — the cross of Christ, the central sign of Christian faith
gold squares radiating outward — divine light emanating from the cross, glory spreading from sacrifice
stepped geometric pattern — the cross as an ordered, cosmic symbol structuring all reality
warm golden tones — the light of God's presence and resurrection glory
description: An abstract mixed-media work on a beige background. At the center, a bold red cross is formed by the intersection of vertical and horizontal bars. Surrounding the cross is a stepped, geometric pattern of golden-brown squares, arranged in a diamond or mandala-like formation radiating outward from the cross. The squares appear to be gold leaf or painted in metallic tones, some with darker patina. The overall shape is symmetrical and centered, creating a luminous, icon-like effect.
theological_description: Oravitzan's abstract composition distills the cross to its essential symbolic power. The red cross at the heart of the golden geometric field proclaims what Paul declared: 'May I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ' (Galatians 6:14). The cross — an instrument of Roman execution — was transformed by Christ's death into the supreme sign of divine love and victory over death. The radiating golden squares suggest light emanating from the cross, fulfilling John's testimony: 'In him was life, and that life was the light of all mankind. The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it' (John 1:4-5). The geometric, mandala-like structure evokes the cosmic dimensions of the cross that Paul describes in Ephesians: the love of Christ that has 'breadth and length and height and depth' (Ephesians 3:18). The abstract, non-figurative treatment reflects the ancient understanding that the cross is not merely a historical artifact but an eternal sign — 'the power of God and the wisdom of God' (1 Corinthians 1:24). The title 'Sign and Light' echoes Simeon's prophecy that the Christ child would be 'a sign that will be opposed' and 'a light for revelation to the Gentiles' (Luke 2:32-34).

scripture_refs.reason: Boasting only in the cross — the cross as the supreme Christian sign depicted at center
The light shining in darkness — golden light radiating from the cross
The message of the cross as the power of God to those being saved
The breadth, length, height, and depth of Christ's love — the cosmic dimensions of the radiating cross
